items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Fulvia! Two large platters please, with two bottles of <te href=850151>Heartpour</te>!

items.speaker: Surprised Septimontian
items.text: Ohh! It's Lupa!

items.speaker: Well-informed Septimontian
items.text: I heard she left the Bough of Legions...

items.speaker: Fulvia
items.text: That's enough, folks. You know the rules, no gossip outside the arena.

items.speaker: Fulvia
items.text: Just dig in!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Still got that fire, huh, Fulvia?

items.speaker: Fulvia
items.text: You should talk. Finally cut ties with the Silvas, eh? With the way they handled the share of profits, you should've left sooner!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Haha… well, back then a strong backer was exactly what I needed. Can't say it was all bad.

items.speaker: Fulvia
items.text: So, this is your new partner. Hmm... Looks like you can handle yourself.

options.text: You can tell just by looking?
The most dependable one in all of Solaris, at your service.

items.speaker: Fulvia
items.text: Can't keep a tavern running for long if you don't have a good eye.

items.speaker: Fulvia
items.text: Well, well, you better prove it in the Agon then.

items.speaker: Fulvia
items.text: By the way, Heartpour is sold out today. Guess the celebrity branding really worked.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: What? Oh, come on...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: What's Heartpour?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: A drink I endorsed. Most of the drinks I promoted were... Ahem. But this one's actually really good.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: If my name's on it, you don't have to worry about quality. It's just that the taste... well, let's just say it's "meh" at best.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Anyway, I'll get you a taste of Heartpour later. For now, let's enjoy the meal!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: So, fellow warrior, any stories to share? There is still much to learn about each other.

items.speaker: Fulvia
items.text: Hey Lupa, got a message from Old Appius. He said the new batch of restored footage just arrived, including those about Contender Mya.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Oh thanks! I almost forgot with everything going on lately.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Restored footage?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Recordings of major matches or famous contenders. Helpful for those who want to learn from them.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Ephor Augusta's are the most popular. Four years ago, she became the champion and a household name by crushing every single opponent. From there, she worked her way up into the Ephor's seat.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: But I prefer watching Contender Mya's matches over any of the past champions.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I think she's... got real heart.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: You can feel it, even through the recordings. The way she threw herself into every fight.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Do you think we'll face her in this Agon?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Ha, not likely. She disappeared during a Great Agon years ago.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Some say she got tangled up in illegal betting scandals. Others think the pressure from her manager drove her to leave Septimont... There are plenty of rumors, but no one knows what really happened.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Still, it's frustrating! To just disappear halfway through...

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: If you fail to make it to the end, people will see you as nothing.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: As expected, the Silvas chose to face us in the semi-finals. The news spreads quickly.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: You knew this was coming?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Of course. To them, it's a provocation... and to forfeit would be a disgrace.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Domina Julia must be here. She wouldn't miss a moment like this.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: She's shrewd and exceptionally capable but arrogant to the core. She's convinced that she's always right.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: She doesn't believe in talking things through, thinks it's for the weak. So we'll show her we don't need rigged matches to win. We'll win with pure stength!

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Four years have passed, and I stand before you once more. Not as a Gladiator, but as a witness to the start of this grand event.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: You all know well that Gladiatorial combat is the lifeblood of our ancient and mighty city.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: "Seize power, and fight for victory."

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Once, we fought to survive on the seas. That same instinct courses through our veins today. The instinct to face fate head-on and defy it.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Remember, those who cling to glory without the strength to defend it will see it slip through their fingers.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Warriors! By the eternal sun, may your courage honor Septimont and prove your loyalty to its spirit!

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I look forward to the rise of new heroes in this Agon.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Let the Agon begin.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Come, my fellow warrior! Victory is ours for the taking!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Esteemed Ephor, I must bring a grave matter to your attention.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: In this very match, the Silvas stooped to underhanded tactics!

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Speak plainly, Gladiator.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: This spear… it was a gift from Domina Julia, head of House Silva, when I first joined the family.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Back then, I trusted the Silvas completely, never once doubting their intentions. That is, until I chose to leave them not long ago.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Now in today's arena, this spear spiraled out of control, nearly stabbing my own teammate!

items.speaker: Julia
items.text: Of course. And your proof?

items.speaker: Julia
items.text: Honorable Ephor, I guarantee this spear will withstand any scrutiny.

items.speaker: Julia
items.text: Lupa, you owe your fame and fortune to our patronage. Yet, you turned your back on us when we needed you the most.

items.speaker: Julia
items.text: Allow me to remind everyone, House Silva does not cower, nor fear defeat. In past Agons, we have constantly led in victories. Even if we lose today, we will not be expelled from Septimont.

items.speaker: Julia
items.text: So, tell me, Lupa, who is truly desperate to destroy whom? A lone Gladiator cut off from her family... or a house as powerful as mine?
Hmph, enough of this disgrace! Stop your performance, jester!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: That's Iuno, the <te href=850152>Priestess</te> of Septimont. She rarely leaves the Temple and only shows up accompanying Ephor Augusta.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: The heroic spirits of the moon and stars have long seen through it. These hollow lies taint Septimont's sacred arena.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Guilty.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Augusta, banish the Silvas from Septimont!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: The Priestess can foretell fate and has the power to make final calls here. Most people trust her judgment.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: But... Iuno is a bit extreme in our line of Priestesses. Not everyone agrees with her behavior, despite her capabilities.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Guilty. The Temple has spoken.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Julia Silva, you know the weight of your actions.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I know your family once stood as the cornerstone of this city. Its pioneers, its founders, its protectors.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: For centuries, the Silvas fought bravely in the hunting grounds, defending Septimont from Tacet Discords.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: But today, you dishonor their legacy. Do not disgrace them further, Julia.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Leave Septimont. Do not ever return.

items.speaker: Julia
items.text: ...As you command, Ephor.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Your performance just now... That wasn't a complete disaster. At least, you show more promise than those idiots retreating to cling to their meager points.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: An outlander... Hmph. Fight well, both of you.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Do not disappoint me or Augusta in tomorrow's final.

items.speaker: Cartur
items.text: Let me cut to the chase. The Silvas are finished.

items.speaker: Cartur
items.text: Ephor Augusta's swift decision sealed their fate. Don't tell me you had no part in this.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: ...Heh, who's to say? How could I predict Domina Julia would tamper with my spear?

items.speaker: Cartur
items.text: Regardless, House Silva has been banished. Even if they deceived you first, you still left their team without a word and violated your contract. You can't deny that.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Skip to the end, Cartur. You're not here to catch up or spread gossip.

items.speaker: Cartur
items.text: Fine, then. I handled the Silvas' sponsorship of the Bough of Legions, and I can ensure the damage to your career is minimal. In return, I'll be your event manager for the next year.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Deal. But we'll need new terms. I trust you'll draw up a contract that satisfies me, Cartur?

items.speaker: Cartur
items.text: Always ready to squeeze an opportunity, aren't you, Lupa?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: The Silvas lit the spark, but you turned a blind eye to what was happening. Don't fool yourself, Cartur. You're complicit in this, too.

items.speaker: Cartur
items.text: ...Fine. We'll do it your way.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Wait. You didn't come to me just for money, did you?

items.speaker: Cartur
items.text: Ephor Augusta, the fiercest hunter in the grounds, our queen. If there's a storm brewing in Septimont, I intend to find the right place to take shelter.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Finally, some peace. What a day!

options.text: At least we made it to the final.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Ephor Augusta had a humble origin. Her path to power was paved in blood.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Decades ago, corruption ran rampant in Septimont, far worse than it is now. Two Ephors met untimely ends back then.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: The Great Agon carries immense weight for the city, and I guess that's why she chose this moment to act.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: The Priestess of Septimont has the authority to make final judgments. Iuno, in particular, is one of Ephor Augusta's staunchest allies.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Most prophets spend their days in the Temple, observing fate's flow and tending to the eternal flames.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: But Iuno's different. She prefers to spend her time in the hunting grounds, bow in hand, patrolling with the other fighters.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Born during an eclipse, she's the most gifted among her line. Impressive, isn't it?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: That's right! I owe you for helping me escape Julia's tricks.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Lupa... It was the Threnodian and Dark Tide's frequencies I sensed in her weapon, if only for a brief moment.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (She thought it was just the Silvas' dirty trick and didn't notice its connection with the Threnodian.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Phrolova said her associate can <color=RedD>weave fate</color> and <color=RedD>stories</color>. If that's true, it means his Forte can influence how things play out.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (But it's unlikely he can freely bend the fabric of reality. The Fractsidus would've overrun Solaris long ago if he could. Either Septimont exists outside reality, or his ability has limits.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (If this isn't the real world, I could take more drastic actions. But if it is, killing Lupa would be an act I could never take back.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (The best approach is to stick to my original plan. Wait for Arsinosa to appear in the final. That's my lead to Cartethyia.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (I also sensed Cartethyia's frequency on Lupa, too. If she's here, she's making moves, just like I am.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (In the meantime, I need to figure out that Overseer's limits. The longer this lasts, the more anomalies are bound to show.)

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: Boss, why the long face huh? Something getting you down?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Buling? Why are you here?

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: I'm here for sightseeing! How could I miss the Great Agon? It's Septimont's signature event!

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: Ah, you are the one to break the deadlock.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: You've noticed it, haven't you, Boss? Septimont is caught in some <color=RedD>anomaly</color>.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: How much do you know about it? This is our second time meeting, Buling. That can't be a coincidence.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: I can "feel" it, but I don't know where it's coming from. Every time I get close, it slips away like smoke.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: But now, I'm sure of it. If I want out of this mess, I need to borrow some of your muscle.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: Oyo, but Taoists have their rules, can't say too much. But helping you helps me... A little nudge is no big deal, right?

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: Here, take this. A little something from me to you. A good start for us. Anyway, it's on me. No charge.

items.speaker: Buling
items.text: Seeing isn't believing, but truth exists in the mirror of the world. Go ahead, see for yourself.

items.speaker: Doubtful Spectator
items.text: What do you think? The final's nearly here. Will Contender Mya claim the title?

items.speaker: Doubtful Spectator
items.text: She's done well so far, but her track record has been shaky...

items.speaker: Anxious Spectator
items.text: She'd better not slip up now!

items.speaker: Anxious Spectator
items.text: I've bet a fortune in the underground pits. If she loses, I-I...

items.speaker: Anxious Spectator
items.text: ...That John fellow promised she'd win no matter what.

items.speaker: Doubtful Spectator
items.text: John? Ah, Mya's manager.

items.speaker: Doubtful Spectator
items.text: Listen, she's got all sorts of talk 'round her, and the odds are crazy. But past champions have always been the noble bloods, haven't they?

items.speaker: Doubtful Spectator
items.text: Not to mention, she's originally from Ragunna...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Buling? Ah, she's gone again.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (I remember Lupa mentioning a Gladiator named Mya. She disappeared years ago during a Great Agon.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Phrolova said the <color=RedD>story</color> only lasts for three days and ends during the final. The Great Agon is at the heart of the story after all.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (The bell on this <te href=850158>mirror</te> is jingling... The closer I move in that direction, the louder it gets. I'd better check it out.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: {PlayerName}!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (For an instant, I felt it. The Threnodian's frequency again.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I've finished talking with them, let's go back. Oh, almost forgot. This is for you!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: The Heartpour I wanted you to try before. Chilled is the best way to have it.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: You need to rest before continuing this investigation. Your strength is spent.

items.speaker: Nameless Girl
items.text: Thank you for your help these past few days.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: We're both from Rinascita. I'm here by the Sentinel's Will. This is simply my duty, for I was once blessed by the Sentinel.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The blessings and freedoms They grant... I wish to share them with everyone across these islands.

items.speaker: Nameless Girl
items.text: The Tidal Blight is spreading faster. I'm afraid—

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: It's okay. Do not let it break you. One thing is certain. This anomaly stems from a specific power. Some facilities and records have been rewound nearly two decades back.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: We just need to find out why and who's responsible.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The clock is ticking, but we still have a chance.

items.speaker: Nameless Girl
items.text: The mastermind behind this will be tricky to deal with.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Don't worry. I'll handle it.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Compared to the darkness that once whispered in my ears, a tangible enemy is easier to face.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Besides, the Righteous One will notice this anomaly when {Male=he;Female=she} arrives. Our moves so far will guide {Male=him;Female=her} forward.

items.speaker: Nameless Girl
items.text: You really trust {Male=him;Female=her}, don't you?

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Yes, very much. We correct each other's mistakes and walk the path of salvation together.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Trust in yourself too. One day, you will reclaim your name.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: As for those vile intentions lurking in the Dark Tide, we'll cut them off together.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Now, shall we continue the investigation? Are you ready?

items.speaker: Nameless Girl
items.text: Absolutely.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Ugh, this is so exhausting! Dealing with these headaches takes more energy than fighting in the arena.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: What is it? You're radiating worry.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: If it's about tomorrow's final, maybe you should relax a bit.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Lupa, can you tell me more about Mya?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Sure, but why the sudden interest?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Just curious. I overheard some people talking about her earlier.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Well, Contender Mya... I first heard about her from Domina Julia.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Julia didn't like her. Probably because they once competed in the same Agon. Mya disappeared after claiming the title, leaving that Agon without an official champion.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: "An ignorant fool." That's how Julia described her. Back then, I'd just been "purchased" by the Silvas, and I got curious about the story.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Turns out, Mya spent almost all her time out of the arena training. Beyond the barest of needs, she devoted herself to honing her craft.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: That kind of dedication wasn't... well-regarded in Septimont back then.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Rumor has it she got caught in dirty match-fixing, cracked under the pressure, and ran.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Everyone has their breaking point. If she ran because of it, I don't blame her.

options.text: What about you? Are you not tired?
But you seem to enjoy gladiating.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Well, maybe I just handle these things better, or maybe I just don't care about people's expectations.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I'm not above the commercial events. It's how I made a name for myself, built my worth. As long as I'm in the arena, I'm happy.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Applause comes and goes. But the things that matter... facilities, resources, opportunities, they don't come cheap.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Septimont has its shadows, but some things still have to get done.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I don't mind being used, as long as whoever I work with knows where the line is.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: I know it's easy to turn a blind eye to the dirty games, but that's just handing Septimont over to the vultures.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: The Silvas played dirty in the arena I hold dear. They trampled over a line that should never be crossed. So we're done.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Look at it... How beautiful.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: To the great City of Septimont.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: {PlayerName}, look over there! That's Arsinosa!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (So that's the Guardian Echo of Septimont...)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (...But there's something different about this Arsinosa compared to the one I saw confronting Cartethyia.)

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Oh... Hold on, I forgot to tell you something yesterday about Mya.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Decades ago, during the Great Agon, Mya disappeared on her way to receive the award. But before that, she did claim the title of the Bloody Agon and even received Arsinosa's blessing.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: The current two-on-two matchup was only introduced thanks to Augusta and her predecessor.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Before that, the matches were... far more brutal.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Gladiators were thrown into a chaotic free-for-all. To hype up the event, the organizers would unleash hordes of Tacet Discords into the arena. The last Gladiator standing was crowned the victor.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: At least, changes have been made since then!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Refined by flames and tempests, all that endures eventually becomes part of Septimont's legacy.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The day of the final is here.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Arsinosa watches, and her significance is known to every Septimontian. It is etched in chronicles and sung in legends.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: If you seek the glory of Arsinosa's blessing, then fight with everything you have!

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Show me if any of you four possess the strength to earn the Guardian's recognition.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Listen, {PlayerName}! Those cheers are for us! They wait for us to give them a fight to remember!

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Are you ready to win this with me?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Wonderful! Now it's hunting time, my fellow warrior!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Lupa...?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: What's wrong? You seem like you're... somewhere else.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Lupa, how did we win?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Er... we defeated our opponents?

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I see...

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (The Bloody Agon I experienced definitely wasn't a dream. It was like the real world <color=RedD>overlapped</color> with the anomalous one.)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (I kept seeing Lupa and Mya <color=RedD>overlap</color> with each other as well.)

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Brilliant performance, warriors.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Remember, this is more than just a title. It is a testament to your own strength and wisdom in overcoming your opponents. You've earned the right to claim it.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: This medal once belonged to a single champion, the last survivor standing in the ring.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: I divided it into two to remind you that...

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Just as we fight alongside Echoes, the glory of Septimont is upheld by heroes who support one another to remain steadfast.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: Fighting alone is commendable. However, true strength lies in protecting those beside you.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: The High Tide draws near. I want all warriors heading to the hunting grounds to remember this.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Hmm? That's interesting.

items.speaker: Iuno
items.text: Augusta, look! Another two have earned Arsinosa's blessing.

items.speaker: Augusta
items.text: You've done incredibly well.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: As a Gladiator, it's a great honor to claim the title with you, {PlayerName}.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Let's savor this moment of glory and joy!

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: You plotted this tragedy.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: No, Madam. I wouldn't take all the credit.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: The architects of this tragedy were the Septimontians themselves. A collective murder so entertainingly woven. With blood on every hand, who's left to point a finger?

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: But I must admit, I'm astonished you would endure all this for strangers and lands you've never set foot on.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: I don't need any grand reason to slay a villain like you. The Threnodian once sought to pull the entire Rinascita into the Dark Tide, but It failed.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: And so will you.

items.speaker: Cristoforo
items.text: Heh... Madam, surely you know you alone cannot undo my story.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Alone? {PlayerName} will come here and find a way to finish this.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: And I will make your so-called perfect play spiral out of control.

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: Silent, hollow words...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: The Dark Tide engulfing the City of Glory...

items.speaker: Cartethyia
items.text: All shall be swept away by the winds at the story's end!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (That fleeting frequency... It was similar to the Threnodian's. Could it really be linked to the Dark Tide Lupa will bring forth in Septimont, as Phrolova said?)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Lupa, can I see your spear?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Here you are!

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (Hmm, I can't feel that abnormal frequency anymore...)

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Lupa, does Domina Julia believe in the Sentinel?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Well. If anything, she despises the Acolytes in Ragunna. Her obsession with titles is for the glory that comes with them.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: She prides herself on being the "perfect Septimontian." It's highly unlikely she'd believe in the Sentinel.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: ...I see.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Sorry, lend me your shoulder for a moment... I'm a bit tired.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Go ahead.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Well, that's settled! Looks like the other two teams have wrapped up their match. The semi-finals are over. Finally, we can leave and get some rest.

items.speaker: Excited Spectator
items.text: Contender Lupa! Congratulations on reaching the final! Oh, and-and you, {PlayerName}, your performance earlier was truly impressive!

items.speaker: Picky Reporter
items.text: Lupa, did you notice any signs of match-fixing involving House Silva before? About that...

items.speaker: Indignant Supporter
items.text: I told you long ago! The Bough of Legions has been exploiting their Gladiators! But no one dared to speak up!

items.speaker: Contemplating Bard
items.text: If you win the final, you'll have the whole of <te href=850156>Solis</te> raising their drinks to your name.

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Alright, alright, everyone! I'll chat with you later. But first, let's give my fellow warrior a moment to breathe, okay?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: {Male=He;Female=She}'s new to Septimont. I'm sure none of you want to get carried away and leave a bad impression on an outlander, hm?

items.speaker: Lupa
items.text: Go. Find somewhere quiet to rest. I'll meet up with you once I've dealt with all this.

items.speaker: Mya
items.text: Relax, Mya. You can do this... Hold on.

items.speaker: Mya
items.text: It's alright... I'll be fine...

items.speaker: Mya
items.text: It... It hasn't been all bad. I've met people who supported me. They cheered me on since I first stepped into the arena...

items.speaker: Mya
items.text: But... I know my limits.

items.speaker: Mya
items.text: No matter how much I push myself, this is as far as I'll go.

items.speaker: Mya
items.text: I love this. Gladiating. Just stepping into the arena makes me feel... happy.

items.speaker: Mya
items.text: But here... I can't treat it like a hobby.

items.speaker: Mya
items.text: And... I want to be seen! To hear them cheer for me! Their laughter, their cheers... I wanna win! I wanna win so, so bad!

items.speaker: Mya
items.text: ...And I need the money.

items.speaker: Mya
items.text: Oh, Divinity above, I hope I won't let anyone down. I need to be better. Much, much better...

items.speaker: Caia
items.text: {Cus:Var,VarType=Global Key=main_team_name} versus the Bough of Legions!

items.speaker: Caia
items.text: Let the Agon begin!

items.speaker: Caia
items.text: {Cus:Var,VarType=Global Key=main_team_name} versus Beasts Beneath!

items.speaker: Caia
items.text: Let the Agon begin!

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: This is but an illusion. One of the night, one of dreams. Not like the spectacle you saw in the glimmer of the Carnevale.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Here, I am not your foe, nor can we harm each other, should we so wish. Lay your blade to rest, if only for now. Tis' a pleasure to see you again, {PlayerName}, on this special stage.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: A city wild and beautiful, chasing endless glory... Tell me, does Septimont please you as much as Ragunna once did?

options.text: Let's cut to the chase. You're connected to Cartethyia's disappearance.
Has the Fractsidus's hand already reached Septimont?

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Ah, so it is she you seek. It appears this special Resonator has you rather captivated.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: When one wrestles against the torrents of fate, every signpost is an anchor guiding you toward the infinite horizon.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Let's take our time. I would like our talk to be pleasant. No one will bother us here.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: I have an associate who adores the heroic tales told throughout this land.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: For this reason...  the missing girl you seek and the anomaly you sensed in Septimont, they are indeed threads in his tapestry.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: You could call it... an obsession of his.

options.text: Stop beating around the bush. Tell me what you really came to say.
Tell me what the Fractsidus is planning.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Every hero's tale begins with a trial.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Your wisdom and courage, your compassion and resolve, your hope and madness... Will they be strong enough to carry you through the trial? To make you the hero of this story?

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Read it. I hope this story speaks to you. The answers you seek lie within these pages.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: If we continue to meet as foes, the symphony grows monotonous. I confess I find it... rather uninspired.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: I'll ask again, Phrolova. What are you doing here in Septimont, and why give me this?

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: I'm giving this story a little nudge.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: As of now, you and that Blessed Maiden from the tower are deeply interwoven in the fate he has spun.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: So you're not the only Overseer active in Rinascita.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: Scar, your nameless ally, and you... The Fractsidus seems to revel in the company of those with twisted obsessions.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: It's not as you imagine. You've already met my associate, you see. He trusts what he sees, not the words of others.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: (I've already crossed paths with him since arriving in Rinascita…?)

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: As a creator, he always hopes his characters will craft twists and turns that he himself cannot foresee.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: He believes that the closer one stands to the protagonist, the greater their influence over the tale.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: But the true lead of this story has yet to emerge.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: I gave you this <te href=850153>play</te> so you can see the ending. That Gladiator girl will be the harbinger of the Dark Tide in Septimont.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: All glory will be consumed, and the pride of heroes will dissolve within a blackened maelstrom. Such is the finale "she" holds most dear.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: This is a short story, {PlayerName}. One that only lasts three days. When the final match concludes, so too will this story.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: You could claim the spotlight by ending her life before the curtain falls. Change the ending, and the tragedy is averted.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: But should you falter, or worse, warn her of what's to come, the consequences will spiral far beyond anything you can imagine.

items.speaker: {PlayerName}
items.text: What you're proposing I do... Tell me, what reason do I have to trust a word you're saying, Phrolova?

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: You don't need to trust me. Let my words slip from your thoughts, if you wish. As I've already said, the story will unfold as my associate has written it.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: The choice lies with you, {PlayerName}. It is a freedom we all hold.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Even the wildest imaginations draw from what already exists.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: So it is with this story. It follows a template, and even its creator cannot escape its confines.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: Choose wisely, {PlayerName}. Time is still on your side, for now.

items.speaker: Phrolova
items.text: I look forward to your performance in this grand finale.
